title: After the HOA
description: After the Coordinator's defeat. The HOA monitors go dark. The suburb flickers, and behind the picket fences the party glimpses gears and brass. A portal opens to the steampunk world. Two rulers. Both used the word 'calibrated.'

lines: The Coordinator's monitors went dark one by one. Not dramatically — sequentially, like a building losing power from the top floor down. Each screen winked out and stayed out.
The HOA office was very quiet. The motivational posters on the walls continued to affirm things. Nobody had told them to stop.
Not the lights. The suburb itself. The picket fences stuttered, and for a half-second behind them: gears. Brass pipes. A sky the color of old copper.
Then the fences were back. The lawns were back. The wrong-shade-of-blue sky was back.
The party stood in the HOA office and looked at where the other world had been.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Gears. Brass. The kind of sky you get when someone decided copper was aspirational.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Two worlds. Two rulers. Mordaine wanted Harmonia calibrated. The Coordinator wanted Maple Heights calibrated. Both used that exact word.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: They didn't just use the same word. They meant the same thing by it. A perfect system with no deviation allowed.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: And they both built their system around us. Mordaine designed the trials. The Coordinator had our files.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Not around us. For us. Or maybe against us. It's difficult to tell the difference from inside.

steps.text: Outside, on the street, a lawn sprinkler turned on and off. Turned on and off. The neighborhood's automation running without anyone to authorize it, or anyone to stop it.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: The next world is going to be the same thing. Different costume, same idea. I'm calling it now.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Someone built this. All of it. Harmonia was a design. The suburb was a design. And now there's a world full of gears behind the fences.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Then we go find the hand.

lines: In the strip mall parking lot, between the frozen yogurt place and the armory that had never explained itself, a shimmer appeared.
Behind it: Cogsworth Junction. A clock tower. A sky that smelled like oil and copper, even through a portal.
The sprinkler ran its cycle and cut off. The neighborhood held its breath, automated and obedient, waiting for someone to tell it what to do next.
Nobody did. The party stepped through.
